pi_items.source: Task description (task.json) explicitly specifies: 'Alert name: exactly ADT Critical Error Monitor'; Verified in verifier.py (line 65) checking exact match; Verified in export_result.sh (line 66) searching for this name in API response

Setup script (setup_task.sh line 86) creates channel with XML element: <name>ADT_Inbound_Processor</name>; Task description explicitly references this channel name; Verified across setup_task.sh and task description
Task description (task.json) explicitly specifies: 'At least one recipient: use ops-team@hospital.example.org with EMAIL protocol'; This is the required email address for alert configuration
Setup script (setup_task.sh lines 78, 84) hardcodes channel ID: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890; Same ID used in deployment (line 186) and saved to /tmp/adt_channel_id.txt (line 192)

Task description specifies: 'Error event types: must trigger on at least SOURCE_CONNECTOR and DESTINATION_CONNECTOR error types'; Verified in verifier.py (lines 94-95) checking for these exact event types
Task description specifies: 'Regex pattern: use .* to catch all errors'
Task description specifies: 'Subject line containing the text ADT'; Verified in verifier.py (line 114) checking if 'ADT' in action_subject
Task description specifies: 'Template/body containing the variable ${error}'; Verified in verifier.py (line 124) checking if '${error}' in action_template
Task description specifies: 'Use the NextGen Connect REST API at https://localhost:8443/api with credentials admin/admin'; Used consistently in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh
Task description specifies: 'All API requests require the header X-Requested-With: OpenAPI'; Used consistently in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh curl requests
Task description specifies: 'credentials admin/admin'; Used in all curl requests in setup_task.sh with -u admin:admin flag
Task description specifies: 'Enabled: true'; Verified in verifier.py (line 76) checking alert_enabled boolean

privileged_info_summary: The configure_error_alert task requires creating an error monitoring alert in NextGen Connect (a real healthcare integration engine) running in a Docker container. The alert must be named 'ADT Critical Error Monitor' and monitor the synthetic channel 'ADT_Inbound_Processor' (with fixed ID a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890) for SOURCE_CONNECTOR and DESTINATION_CONNECTOR errors using regex pattern '.*'. The alert's action group must include a subject containing 'ADT', a template with the ${error} variable, and at least one recipient (ops-team@hospital.example.org) using EMAIL protocol. All API calls use the endpoint https://localhost:8443/api with credentials admin/admin and require the 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI' header.
